rulings.Case Title: GARY BROWN, ET AL VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Brayton Purcell¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiffs is denied as untimely. Brayton Purcell failed to provide proper notice as required by C.C.P. 1005(b). Service by mail on April 17, 2013 to an address in Idaho with a May 16, 2013 hearing date did not provide sufficient notice to plaintiffs pursuant to C.C.P. 1005(b). The motion is denied without prejudice to being re-filed as long as proper notice is provided to plaintiffs. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM THOMAS, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR AND AS VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Thomas was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Verduzco¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. Even if the court considers Mr. Verduzco¿s declaration, or even if the court had continued this hearing to allow defendant to depose Mr. Verduzco and Mr. Verduzco affirmed the statements made in his declaration, it would be irrelevant because plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether the insulation at issue contained asbestos. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
